sutta: Chief among those of great wisdom is Sāriputta chief among those of supernormal powers is the great Moggallāna chief among those who are clairvoyant is anuruddha chief among those who receive offerings is Sīvalī chief among those of retentive memory is Ānanda chief among those who know the Vinaya is Upāli chief among the nuns of long standing is maha Pajāpati Gotamī chief among the nuns of great wisdom is Khemā chief among the nuns of supernormal powers is Uppalavaṇṇā chief among the nuns proficient in Vinaya is Paṭācārā chief among lay followers who are alms givers is anathapindika chief among lay followers who are Dhamma teachers is Citta chief among lay women followers who minister to the sangha is Visākhā megara's mother chief among lay women who nurse the sick is supiya.
commentary: In this 1.14 There are many the disciples of the Buddha who are mentioned the monks and nuns as well as lay men and lay women and eminent ones are mentioned those who are known for something so I won't mention all of them i'll just mention a few of them among the monks the Buddha always praises Sāriputta in theravada in mahayana sutras Sāriputta is sometimes portrayed as having shallow wisdom this is seen in the vimalakirti sutra anuruddha had the heavenly eye and could see the whole world system clearly Sīvalī is known for receiving many offerings people keep his image hoping for merit Ānanda had the best memory and recited the discourses after the Buddha's passing Upāli was foremost in Vinaya discipline maha Pajāpati Gotamī was the Buddha's stepmother she nursed the Buddha after his birth she later became the first nun after Ānanda interceded Khemā was formerly a queen and became foremost in wisdom among nuns Uppalavaṇṇā had great psychic powers and could visit the Buddha even at midnight arahants were allowed to see the Buddha directly Paṭācārā had a tragic life and became an arahant after meeting the Buddha anathapindika built a monastery and supported monks daily Visākhā also supported thousands of monks Citta was a lay follower who attained high states and could teach monks Visākhā was called megara's mother after teaching her father in law supiya once cut flesh from her own thigh to feed a sick monk the Buddha healed her wound instantly.
